title: External secrets authorization bypass: credential save resolves $secrets without externalSecret:list check
description: When saving (creating or updating) a credential, n8n's CredentialsService validates the payload through checkCredentialData (and prepareUpdateData), which only enforced mandatory-field presence and OAuth URL validation. It performed no authorization check on external-secret references, so a user could embed an external-secret expression ($secrets.<vault>.<key>) in a credential field; the value was resolved from the connected vault and stored, returning the plaintext secret to a user lacking the externalSecret:list permission. This bypassed the externalSecret:list authorization scope (CWE-863-style missing object/permission-level authorization) and required only that an external secrets vault be configured and the attacker know or guess a secret name.
